name: Triceps Pushdown
resistance_profile: Loads the triceps in the shortened/mid range; complements overhead (lengthened) work.
rom_notes: Full extension to a controlled deep flexion at the top.
cues: Pin the upper arms to the sides
execution_steps: Set a cable to the top and grip a straight bar, angled bar, or rope at about shoulder width.
Pin your upper arms to your sides and hinge slightly forward.
Extend the elbows fully, driving the bar down until the arms are straight.
Control the return until the forearms are just above parallel, keeping the elbows pinned.
good_when: You want an easy-to-learn, joint-friendly triceps isolation.
Adding high-rep pump work or an end-of-session finisher.
bad_when: You want to emphasize the stretched long head (use an overhead extension).
You are chasing heavy compound triceps overload (use the close-grip bench press).
common_errors: Leaning in to press with the torso
